_comment: Seed order = editor's ranking; live order re-sorts by community upvotes (btb_sunset_spot_counts). walk_min is from the top of Church Street.

spots.name: Waterfront Park
spots.why: The default for a reason — front row on the boardwalk, the whole western sky over the lake, five minutes downhill from Church Street.

spots.name: Battery Park
spots.why: Elevation is the trick: you see over the treetops to the full Adirondack ridgeline, and the cannons make a good leaning post.

spots.name: Oakledge Park
spots.why: Flat rock ledges that hold the day's heat — sit on warm stone while the sky does its thing. Quieter than the boardwalk.

spots.name: North Beach
spots.why: Sand under your feet and the widest open horizon in town. On hot nights you can be in the water when the color peaks.

spots.name: Colchester Causeway
spots.why: Three miles of marble spit with water on both sides — the only spot where the sunset surrounds you. Bring a bike and lights for the ride back.

spots.name: Perkins Pier
spots.why: Sailboat masts in the foreground turn every photo into a postcard, and it stays calm when the boardwalk is packed.

spots.name: Red Rocks Park
spots.why: Short pine-woods walk to 70-foot cliffs over the broad lake — the most dramatic perch in the area when the sky delivers.

spots.name: Leddy Park
spots.why: The locals' quiet option: same western horizon as North Beach with a fraction of the crowd, and easy parking.
